ANIMAL BITE — RABIES EXPOSURE CHECKLIST
Based on Circular H80/2024 & NICD Advisory (June 2024)
⚠ This tool is for clinical decision support only. It is not a substitute for clinical judgement. For use by trained healthcare personnel. Please verify against current NICD/national guidelines before acting. The tool is not officially endorsed by NDoH, NICD, WCG, or VHW.
© vic-ec. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0 — free to use and share with attribution; commercial use, resale, or profit from this material is not permitted.
⚠ ACTIVE OUTBREAK ALERT — CAPE FUR SEAL RABIES (June 2024)
Rabies confirmed in Cape Fur seals (Big Bay, Muizenberg, Strand). All seal bites back to 1 December 2023 require retrospective PEP assessment. Seal bites are managed identically to dog bites. A Noordhoek dog has also been confirmed rabies-positive following suspected seal contact.
Step 1A — Select The Animal Involved
Step 1B — Select the Type of Exposure
CATEGORY I
No skin breach
No direct contact, or touching/feeding animal with intact skin
e.g. petting a potentially rabid animal, being in its presence
CATEGORY II
Contact — No bleeding
Direct contact but NO breach of skin, NO bleeding
e.g. superficial scratch, nibbling, bruising without blood
CATEGORY III
Breach of skin / mucosal
Any bleeding, lick on broken skin/mucosa, or any bat contact
e.g. bite with blood, lick on wound, lick in eyes/mouth, any bat
Step 1C — Select the Exposure Severity
Tick all features that apply in either column — at least one selection required to proceed
⚠ HIGH RISK
✓ LOW RISK
Source Animal
Type of Contact
Location
Patient Factors
  • Symptomatic HIV, malignancy on immunosuppressive agents, high dose steroids >20 mg/day
⚠ Disclaimer: Low-risk features do not eliminate the need for clinical assessment. Any uncertainty should prompt discussion with the ID Specialist on call before withholding PEP.
Tygerberg: 021 938 4911  |  Groote Schuur: 021 404 9111  |  NICD: 0800 212 552
02 CATEGORY I — Low Risk LOW RISK
No breach of skin. Being in presence of a rabid animal, or touching/petting with intact skin.
✓ No Rabies vaccine or RIG required for Category I exposure
Wound Management
  • Wash wound thoroughly with soap and water for 5–10 minutes — do this NOW before any other intervention
  • Follow with chlorhexidine 0.05%and then iodine solution/ointment
  • Tetanus prophylaxis is not routinely indicated for intact-skin contact, but assess tetanus status if the history of skin integrity is uncertain.
Patient Instructions
  • Reassure patient — no skin breach occurred and no rabies transmission risk exists
  • Advise patient to return if any new symptoms or concerns arise
Contact State Veterinarian
  • Contact State Veterinarian to report animal if indicated (see contacts below)
02 CATEGORY II — Medium Risk CONSIDER VACCINE
Direct contact, NO breach of skin, NO bleeding — e.g. superficial scratch, nibble without blood.
Wound Management
  • Wash wound thoroughly with soap and water for 5–10 minutes — do this NOW before any other intervention
  • Follow with chlorhexidine 0.05%and then iodine solution/ointment
  • Avoid local anaesthetic agents if possible (may spread virus locally)
Tetanus Status
  • Assess tetanus status — provide tetanus vaccination if indicated
    i.e. last booster >5 years ago, uncertain immunisation history, or unvaccinated
Rabies Vaccine
  • Consider Rabies vaccine in high risk cases — 4 doses: Days 0, 3, 7, and between Days 14–28
Does patient require Rabies Vaccine?
VACCINE SCHEDULE & ADMINISTRATION
Calculate schedule — Date of 1st dose: ⚠ Date required
DoseDayCalculated DateRouteSite
1stDay 0—IM injectionDeltoid (adults) / anterolateral thigh (children <2 yrs)
⚠ NEVER gluteus maximus — INEFFECTIVE
2ndDay 3—
3rdDay 7—
4thDay 14–28—
One vial = one dose (regardless of vial size) for adults and children. Changing vaccine brand during a course is acceptable if unavoidable.
Previously vaccinated patient: If reliable vaccination history — give booster doses only on Days 0 and 3. No RIG needed.
Rabies Immunoglobulin
  • No RIG required for Category II
⚠ SIGNIFICANT IMMUNOSUPPRESSION PRESENT. DISCUSSION WITH ID SPECIALIST ADVISED
Category II wounds in patients with significant immunosuppression may warrant RIG in addition to vaccine. Seek ID Specialist advice before proceeding.
Tygerberg ID: 021 938 4911  |  Groote Schuur ID: 021 404 9111  |  NICD: 0800 212 552
RIG required? (Significant immunosuppression — seek ID advice)
RIG DOSE CALCULATOR & ADMINISTRATION — HIGH RISK CATEGORY II
RIG DOSE CALCULATOR
kg ⚠ Weight required
Human RIG (HRIG)
20 IU/kg
IU — preferred product
Vials Required
300 IU/vial
vials (HRIG, rounded up)
Equine RIG (ERIG)
40 IU/kg
IU
RIG ADMINISTRATION
#StepDetail
1Identify wound(s)Locate all bite/scratch sites. Ensure wound has been washed before RIG infiltration.
2Draw up doseCalculate dose (20 IU/kg HRIG or 40 IU/kg ERIG). Do not exceed calculated dose.
3Infiltrate woundInject as much as anatomically possible directly into and around the wound margins. Use a fine needle; fan out from wound edges.
4Multiple woundsDilute RIG with equal volume normal saline to ensure all wounds can be infiltrated. Prioritise the highest-risk wound if volume is limiting.
5Separate from vaccineUse a different syringe and different anatomical site from vaccine dose — RIG inactivates vaccine if mixed.
6TimingGive on Day 0 with first vaccine dose. Do not give after Day 7 from first vaccine dose.
RIG administration rules:
• Infiltrate as much as anatomically possible into and around the wound — no additional IM injection elsewhere
• Multiple wounds: dilute RIG in equal volumes saline and infiltrate all wounds
• Give RIG in a different syringe and at a different site from vaccine (RIG inactivates vaccine)
• Do NOT exceed recommended dose (immune suppression risk)
• RIG must be given within 7 days of first vaccine dose — if unavailable now, start vaccine and source RIG urgently
Previously vaccinated patient: No RIG needed. Give booster vaccine on Days 0 and 3 only.
Patient Instructions
  • If vaccine administered, issue patient with written record of doses received and dates for future vaccinations (for other facilities). Ensure patient understands importance of completing all 4 doses.
Contact State Veterinarian
  • Contact State Veterinarian to report animal if indicated (see contacts below)
02 CATEGORY III — High Risk HIGH RISK — VACCINE + RIG
Breach of skin + any bleeding; lick on broken skin or mucous membranes (eyes, nose, mouth); any bat contact. This is a medical emergency.
Wound Management
  • Wash wound thoroughly with soap and water for 5–10 minutes — do this NOW before any other intervention
  • Follow with chlorhexidine 0.05%and then iodine solution/ointment
  • Avoid local anaesthetic agents if possible (may spread virus locally)
  • Delay or avoid suturing where possible (suturing may spread virus locally)
  • Consider antibiotics (e.g. amoxicillin-clavulanate) if clinically indicated — especially for hand or face bites, deep puncture wounds, or immunocompromised patients
Tetanus Status
  • Assess tetanus status — provide tetanus vaccination if indicated
    i.e. last booster >5 years ago, uncertain immunisation history, or unvaccinated
Rabies Vaccine
  • Strongly consider Rabies vaccine in high risk cases — 4 doses: Days 0, 3, 7, and between Days 14–28. Do not delay pending specialist review.
Does patient require Rabies Vaccine?
VACCINE SCHEDULE & ADMINISTRATION
Calculate schedule — Date of 1st dose: ⚠ Date required
DoseDayCalculated DateRouteSite
1stDay 0—IM injectionDeltoid (adults) / anterolateral thigh (children <2 yrs)
⚠ NEVER gluteus maximus — INEFFECTIVE
2ndDay 3—
3rdDay 7—
4thDay 14–28—
One vial = one dose (regardless of vial size) for adults and children. Changing vaccine brand during a course is acceptable if unavoidable.
Previously vaccinated patient: If reliable vaccination history — give booster doses only on Days 0 and 3. No RIG needed.
Rabies Immunoglobulin
  • RIG is indicated for high-risk Category III exposure. Contact ID Specialist urgently if any uncertainty or if RIG is unavailable.
  • RIG may be withheld only if: patient has previously completed a full PEP or pre-exposure course with documented reliable history, OR after explicit discussion with ID Specialist.
Does patient require RIG?
RIG DOSE CALCULATOR & ADMINISTRATION
RIG DOSE CALCULATOR
kg ⚠ Weight required
Human RIG (HRIG)
20 IU/kg
IU — preferred product
Vials Required
300 IU/vial
vials (HRIG, rounded up)
Equine RIG (ERIG)
40 IU/kg
IU — if HRIG unavailable
RIG ADMINISTRATION
#StepDetail
1Identify wound(s)Locate all bite/scratch/lick sites. Ensure wound has been washed before RIG infiltration.
2Draw up doseCalculate dose (20 IU/kg HRIG or 40 IU/kg ERIG). Do not exceed calculated dose.
3Infiltrate woundInject as much as anatomically possible directly into and around the wound margins using a fine needle. Fan out from wound edges. For mucous membrane exposure, apply RIG topically where infiltration is not possible.
4Multiple woundsDilute RIG with equal volume normal saline to ensure all wounds are infiltrated. Prioritise the highest-risk wound if volume is limiting.
5Separate from vaccineUse a different syringe and different anatomical site from vaccine dose — RIG inactivates vaccine if mixed or given at same site.
6TimingGive on Day 0 with first vaccine dose. Do not give after Day 7 from first vaccine dose — no benefit beyond this point.
7If RIG unavailableStart vaccine immediately and source RIG urgently. Contact ID Specialist and NICD hotline. Document delay in patient record.
RIG administration rules:
• Infiltrate as much as anatomically possible into and around the wound — no additional IM injection elsewhere
• Multiple wounds: dilute RIG in equal volumes saline and infiltrate all wounds
• Give RIG in a different syringe and at a different site from vaccine (RIG inactivates vaccine)
• Do NOT exceed recommended dose (immune suppression risk)
• RIG must be given within 7 days of first vaccine dose — if unavailable now, start vaccine and source RIG urgently
Previously vaccinated patient: No RIG needed. Give booster vaccine on Days 0 and 3 only.
Patient Instructions
  • If vaccine administered, issue patient with written record of doses received and dates for future vaccinations (for other facilities). Ensure patient understands importance of completing all 4 doses.
Contact State Veterinarian
  • Contact State Veterinarian to report animal if indicated (see contacts below)
02b SEAL BITE — Additional Considerations OUTBREAK
  • Seal bites since 1 December 2023 require retrospective exposure reassessment — apply PEP according to the exposure category determined above
  • If bitten within last 6 weeks: RIG into wound + 4 doses vaccine (even if wound healed, infiltrate RIG into wound site)
  • If bitten more than 6 weeks ago: 4 doses vaccine only (no RIG)
  • Contact ID Specialist for all seal bites (Tygerberg 021-938-4911 or GSH 021-404-9111)
  • Complete WC Animal Bite Incident Form and email to [email protected]
03 Escalation — When to Call for Advice IMPORTANT
  • All seal bites — contact ID Specialist
  • High-risk exposures where PEP indication is uncertain
  • Immunocompromised patients, pregnant women, children — special considerations apply
  • If RIG not available — start vaccine, attempt to source RIG within 7 days of 1st vaccine dose
  • Contact State Veterinarian to arrange animal assessment if possible

04 Documentation & Reporting — Required Forms MANDATORY
  • ALL BITES
    WC Animal Bite Incident Form (Annexure 2, June 2024)
    Complete for ALL new animal bite cases AND retrospective seal bites since Dec 2023. Email to: [email protected]
  • PATIENT RECORD
    Vaccine with/without RIG administration record
    Document all prophylaxis and immunoglobulin administration on patient file AND issue patient with written record of doses received + dates (for use at other facilities/provinces).
  • PHARMACY
    Vaccine/RIG requisition to pharmacy
    Notify pharmacy services to order stock promptly if low. Monitor stock and act early as demand rises.
